diagrams.title: Risk Identification Process Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the systematic process of identifying risks in software engineering projects, from project initialization through risk discovery techniques like brainstorming, documentation review, and past project analysis.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the systematic approach software teams use to identify potential risks before they become issues, emphasizing that identification is an ongoing process throughout the project lifecycle.

diagrams.title: Risk Assessment Matrix (Projection)
diagrams.description: A 3x3 probability-impact matrix grid used to project and categorize identified risks. Shows how risks are plotted based on likelihood of occurrence (Low/Medium/High) versus potential impact on the project (Low/Medium/High).
elements: 9 grid cells with color coding (Green=Low risk, Yellow=Medium risk, Red=High risk)
Sample risk items plotted in appropriate cells
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how software teams project and prioritize risks by evaluating both the likelihood of a risk occurring and its potential damage, enabling informed mitigation resource allocation.

diagrams.title: Risk Mitigation Workflow
diagrams.description: A cyclical process diagram showing the continuous flow from risk identification through mitigation strategies: Avoid, Mitigate, Transfer, and Accept. Shows the feedback loop where mitigation outcomes inform future identification.
elements: Mitigation Strategy node (Avoid, Mitigate, Transfer, Accept)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the iterative nature of risk management in software engineering, showing that mitigation is not a one-time activity but a continuous cycle of identification, assessment, action, and monitoring.
